what_happened: ILM moves from concept X-wings to shootable filming miniatures, working out fuselage bucks, armatures, wing mechanisms, and revised shell geometry.
known_people: David Beasley; Grant McCune; David Grell; Joe Johnston; Dave Jones and others
filming_relation: development of the final ANH X-wing miniature family

what_happened: The first completed ILM X-wing is finished, detailed, and shipped to John Barry in England.
known_people: Lorne Peterson; Jon Erland; Steve Gawley; Joe Johnston; John Barry
filming_relation: used as cockpit / set reference before its final ANH hero state

what_happened: After returning from England, Blue 1 is completed with cockpit, pilot, and electrical finishing and redressed into Red 2.
known_people: Grant McCune; Joe Johnston; Steve Gawley and team
filming_relation: becomes a finished hero filming miniature

what_happened: The X-wing program expands into a mixed fleet of bespoke hero miniatures, high-finish pyros, and rougher destruction stand-ins.
known_people: Van Nuys model shop; mold shop; stage crew
filming_relation: active miniature photography and destruction work through the main battle-effects phase

what_happened: The first ILM Y-wing exists by December 1975 and is sent incomplete to England so the full-size prop builders can blueprint and mock up the 1:1 set piece.
known_people: early Y-wing crew; likely Joe Johnston / model-shop staff
filming_relation: reference support rather than a securely documented finished ANH hero state

what_happened: Real Y-wing build work is underway by spring 1976, with Paul Huston building the master pattern / hero buck used to generate the ANH Y-wing family.
known_people: Paul Huston and model-shop crew
filming_relation: pattern source for later filming miniatures

what_happened: High-finish Y-wing miniatures are in service for trench and formation work, though the exact object-to-callsign mapping is still partly contested.
known_people: Paul Huston and model-shop crew
filming_relation: active hero filming miniatures for the battle program

what_happened: A separate Y-wing pyro family exists alongside the surviving high-finish ships.
known_people: model shop; mold shop; stage crew
filming_relation: destruction stand-ins rather than the preserved hero survivors

what_happened: A dated maroon two-tone TIE test miniature is photographed under motion-blur / compositing conditions.
known_people: original ILM test crew
filming_relation: camera / compositing test proving TIE shooting is active by this date

what_happened: After the test phase, the ANH TIE program settles into the familiar light-grey family, including marked and damaged states.
known_people: Van Nuys model shop and stage crew
filming_relation: standard battle miniatures used in motion-control photography

what_happened: Dedicated TIE pyrotechnic units are built for shot-down explosion work.
known_people: model shop; pyro / stage crew
filming_relation: destruction stand-ins separate from standard stage-shot TIEs

what_happened: A production blueprint signed Johnston 9/25/75 anchors the Escape Pod as one of the earliest precisely dated ANH miniature designs.
known_people: Joe Johnston
filming_relation: earliest firm date anchor for this miniature line

subject_or_object: Escape Pod miniature and launch setup
what_happened: The main Escape Pod miniature and some launch-specific handling or rigging setup must exist by the time active Death Star and ship miniature work is underway, but the exact build and filming dates are still open.
known_people: Joe Johnston and model-shop crew
filming_relation: real filming miniature plus probable shot-specific launch support arrangement

what_happened: Paul Huston says the Death Star cannon, built with Stuart Ziff, was the key model in the first completed ILM shot.
known_people: Paul Huston; Stuart Ziff
filming_relation: early functioning-stage photography anchor

what_happened: The Death Star assault work relies on one main sphere plus a large interlocking system of surface pieces, low-altitude tiles, and related panel families.
known_people: broad Death Star build crew
filming_relation: active miniature system for approach trench and battle shots

what_happened: The trench program includes more than generic panels: gun towers, trench backwall lighting, the one-shot crane, silo / tower pieces, and the docking-bay gantry family.
known_people: specific builders partly open; local attribution ties the gantry to Jon Erland
filming_relation: special sections used in close trench and docking-bay miniature work

what_happened: A larger detailed Blockade Runner is part of the ANH model program, distinct from the small recession model.
known_people: exact build team still open; later provenance touches Grant McCune and Richard Edlund circles
filming_relation: likely used for the main detailed photography though shot-to-model mapping is still incomplete

what_happened: A unique 16-inch Blockade Runner is built to create the illusion of recession in the opening shot because of limited track length.
known_people: Richard Edlund cited in provenance trail; Grant McCune collection provenance
filming_relation: built for one tightly defined opening-shot job

what_happened: At least two ANH Landspeeder miniatures existed apart from the full-size prop, but their exact states and build chronology remain open.
known_people: ILM vehicle miniature crew not yet pinned
filming_relation: real ANH miniatures but still weakly dated in the current evidence set

what_happened: The Sandcrawler is remembered by Paul Huston as a model almost everyone touched once the shop hit a more efficient production rhythm.
known_people: Paul Huston; broad model-shop crew
filming_relation: one of the clearest shared-shop builds in the ANH program
